recurringPattern: Linear progress bar (thin horizontal line, brand color fill on gray track) or a circular determinate ring showing percentage. Often used in multi-step forms, file uploads, and loading states. The bar always animates smoothly even for instantaneous operations.
designHarm: The horizontal progress bar is used universally for all progression metaphors — multi-step forms, upload progress, subscription activation — though each has different user expectations about duration and completion. Indeterminate bars (animated stripes/dots) appear for operations that can't measure progress, providing false reassurance. The smooth animation adds visual delight but delays the user's perception of completion.
legitimateExceptions: File upload/download where byte-level progress is measurable and meaningful
Multi-wizard forms where step number out of total is the primary user question
possibleFixtures: Standard linear progress bar with percentage
Step indicator with numbered stages instead of bar
Non-linear progression display for indeterminate operations
originalObservations: AI-generated progress bars consistently use the brand's primary color as the fill on a light gray track.
The bar height is always 4-8px regardless of the context or importance of the progress.
Smooth transition animations (width transitions) are applied to every bar even when the progress is computed in discrete steps.
Indeterminate progress uses an animated striped/dotted pattern identical to CSS loading patterns.
